verses.1: Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good, For His mercy is everlasting.
verses.2: The redeemed of the LORD shall say so, Those whom He has redeemed from the hand of the enemy
verses.3: And gathered from the lands, From the east and from the west, From the north and from the south.
verses.4: They wandered in the wilderness in a desert region; They did not find a way to an inhabited city.
verses.5: They were hungry and thirsty; Their souls felt weak within them.
verses.6: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He saved them from their distresses.
verses.7: He also had them walk on a straight way, To go to an inhabited city.
verses.8: They shall give thanks to the LORD for His mercy, And for His wonders to the sons of mankind!
verses.9: For He has satisfied the thirsty soul, And He has filled the hungry soul with what is good.
verses.10: There were those who lived in darkness and in the shadow of death, Prisoners in misery and chains,
verses.11: Because they had rebelled against the words of God And rejected the plan of the Most High.
verses.12: Therefore He humbled their heart with labor; They stumbled and there was no one to help.
verses.13: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He saved them from their distresses.
verses.14: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death And broke their bands apart.
verses.15: They shall give thanks to the LORD for His mercy, And for His wonders to the sons of mankind!
verses.16: For He has shattered gates of bronze And cut off bars of iron.
verses.17: Fools, because of their rebellious way, And because of their guilty deeds, were afflicted.
verses.18: Their souls loathed all kinds of food, And they came close to the gates of death.
verses.19: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He saved them from their distresses.
verses.20: He sent His word and healed them, And saved them from their destruction.
verses.21: They shall give thanks to the LORD for His mercy, And for His wonders to the sons of mankind!
verses.22: They shall also offer sacrifices of thanksgiving, And tell of His works with joyful singing.
verses.23: Those who go down to the sea in ships, Who do business on great waters;
verses.24: They have seen the works of the LORD, And His wonders in the deep.
verses.25: For He spoke and raised a stormy wind, Which lifted the waves of the sea.
verses.26: They rose up to the heavens, they went down to the depths; Their soul melted away in their misery.
verses.27: They reeled and staggered like a drunken person, And were at their wits’ end.
verses.28: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble, And He brought them out of their distresses.
verses.29: He caused the storm to be still, So that the waves of the sea were hushed.
verses.30: Then they were glad because they were quiet, So He guided them to their desired harbor.
verses.31: They shall give thanks to the LORD for His mercy, And for His wonders to the sons of mankind!
verses.32: They shall also exalt Him in the congregation of the people, And praise Him at the seat of the elders.
verses.33: He turns rivers into a wilderness, And springs of water into a thirsty ground;
verses.34: And a fruitful land into a salt waste, Because of the wickedness of those who dwell in it.
verses.35: He turns a wilderness into a pool of water, And a dry land into springs of water;
verses.36: And He has the hungry live there, So that they may establish an inhabited city,
verses.37: And sow fields and plant vineyards, And gather a fruitful harvest.
verses.38: He also blesses them and they multiply greatly, And He does not let their cattle decrease.
verses.39: When they become few and lowly Because of oppression, misery, and sorrow,
verses.40: He pours contempt upon noblemen And makes them wander in a pathless wasteland.
verses.41: But He sets the needy securely on high, away from affliction, And makes his families like a flock.
verses.42: The upright see it and are glad; But all injustice shuts its mouth.
verses.43: Who is wise? He is to pay attention to these things, And consider the mercy of the LORD.
